TextENGNew: ``......Five minutes only, understood?@k@vS02/00/doc03002. Please try not to agitate him.``
``Of course, of course. I know the drill.``
Click.
The door opened, and... just as I expected, Ooishi-san walked in.
``Well, well, Maebara-san.@k@vS02/11/130700394. It's been quite a while, hasn't it?@k@vS02/11/130700395. How have you been feeling since then?``
``...The doctors say my recovery from the surgery is going well, but... I feel like seeing your face just set me back.``
``Bwa-ha-ha-ha-ha! That's harsh!``
Ooishi-san let out a sweaty laugh and handed Mom a gift box of assorted sweets he'd apparently picked up from the souvenir shop on the hospital's first floor.
``Pardon me, ma'am.@k@vS02/11/130700398. Would you mind if I had a brief word with Keiichi-kun?``
changeReason: Old says 'Mrs. Maebara'; new says 'ma'am.' JPN お母さん is a general address meaning 'mother/ma'am' without using the family name.

TextENGNew: Mom made no effort to hide her displeasure at the idea of a police officer speaking to her son without her present.
...But I spoke up too.
``...Mom.@k@vS02/01/130101188. Sorry, but just for a little while.@k@vS02/01/130101189. Five minutes is all I need.``
Reluctantly, Mom left the room.
Once the door closed, Ooishi-san broke into a wide grin and surveyed the hospital room.
``A private room! Living the high life, aren't we?@k@vS02/11/130700400. My old lady always ends up in a shared room, you know.@k@vS02/11/130700401. Quite the lively scene in there, let me tell you.``

``In places like that, the severity of your illness becomes a kind of status symbol, apparently.@k@vS02/11/130700403. The sicker you are, the more clout you carry... or maybe the more you lord it over everyone...``
``...Ooishi-san, we have five minutes.@k@vS02/01/130101191. I'd prefer these to be the very last five minutes we ever spend together.``
``...I have nothing to do with Hinamizawa anymore... and this place isn't Hinamizawa, either.``
``...Well, there aren't any general hospitals in Okinomiya, after all.@k@vS02/11/130700405. ...Mind if I sit?@k@vS02/11/130700406. There we go.``
This was a large university hospital in Shishibone City.
A proper urban setting, worlds apart from the likes of Hinamizawa or Okinomiya.
It was hard to believe this was even in the same municipality as Hinamizawa.
``When do you think you'll be transferred?``
``Who knows.@k@vS02/01/130101194. ...I'd be fine going anytime, honestly.``
My severe injuries had delayed our family's move somewhat.
My parents had already vacated the house in Hinamizawa and were settled into the new place.
Once I'd recovered enough to be transported, the plan was to transfer me to a hospital near our new home.
``...Please don't come looking for me at the new hospital.@k@vS02/01/130101196. I really will take a turn for the worse.``

``Oh dear... I've been completely written off.@k@vS02/11/130700409. It stings, even if it does come with the territory.@k@vS02/11/130700410. That's why today I've brought all sorts of lovely gifts to make amends.``
``...You really didn't need to bring me a tin of cookies.``
``Ah, but you see~@k@vS02/11/130700412. There actually aren't cookies inside.@k@vS02/11/130700413. Ta-da~``
Ooishi-san popped open the cookie tin, and... wha?!@k Ow, ow, my surgical wound is pulling...

``Since I didn't know your tastes, Maebara-san, I brought a whole variety~@k@vS02/11/130700415. Look here, a smorgasbord for every palate, from Westerns to comics!@k@vS02/11/130700416. Go on, take as many as you like~!``
``D-D-Don't bring that kind of stuff in here~!!!@k@vS02/01/130101199. Take them back, take them back!!@k@vS02/01/130101200. Ow ow ow ow...!!``
changeReason: Old says 'porn'; new says 'that kind of stuff.' JPN こんな本 (this kind of books) doesn't explicitly say porn; old adds specificity not in the original.

TextENGNew: ``Heh-heh-heh!@k@vS02/11/130700418. I figured a healthy young man like you, cooped up in a place like this for sooo long, would be in dire need.@k@vS02/11/130700419. Stay here much longer and the only thing you'll develop a taste for is nurses, you know?``

``There's so much more to appreciate in this world besides nurses, after all!@k@vS02/11/130700421. Don't specialize too early—keep your horizons broad!@k@vS02/11/130700422. You've got to be an all-rounder.@k@vS02/11/130700423. Heh-heh-heh-heh-heh!!``
I managed a pained grin to match Ooishi-san's bawdy laughter.
...But somehow, I understood.
When Ooishi-san started a conversation this way... it meant he undoubtedly had something important to discuss.
``...............What happened to Shion-san was truly unfortunate.``
The night I was stabbed by Mion.
Shion had fallen from a balcony and died.
...The police said they were investigating all possibilities—accident, suicide, homicide—but... it could only have been Mion's doing...
Mion had tracked down Shion's hiding place... and pushed her to her death.@k There was no other explanation.
``...............It was no accident.@k@vS02/01/130101203. ...It was Mion.@k@vS02/01/130101204. ...Without a doubt.``

``...The forensics team insists it could only have been suicide, but I share your opinion, Maebara-san.@k@vS02/11/130700426. ...She pushed Shion-san off the balcony, then stabbed you.@k@vS02/11/130700427. ...All in a single night.``
changeReason: Old says 'criminology guys' but 鑑識の連中 means 'forensics/crime scene team.'

TextENGNew: ``.....................Has Mion... still not been found?``
``Ah, yes.@k@vS02/11/130700429. That's actually what I came to tell you today.@k@vS02/11/130700430. ...Yes.``
``She has, in fact, been found.``
...Hearing that... stirred something strange inside me.

Part of me felt relieved that the incident was truly over... and part of me felt sorry that my closest friend had finally been caught.@k ...And so many other things besides...@k ...My heart was a tangle of emotions.
``That's not all.@k@vS02/11/130700433. In the Sonozaki estate's underground.@k@vS02/11/130700434. ...We found a hidden well.``
``Just as you told us, Maebara-san, the remains of every missing person were found at the bottom.``
...The faint possibility I'd clung to—the hope that Rika-chan and Satoko might still be alive, sustained only by the fact that their bodies hadn't been found—was gone.
``It was remarkably well concealed down in that underground.@k@vS02/11/130700437. A blind spot within a blind spot, truly.``
``It was a vertical shaft, dug out like a well.@k@vS02/11/130700439. ...A ladder was installed inside, and when you climbed all the way down, there was a horizontal tunnel branching off.``
``...Crawl a few hundred meters through that, and you'd come out at an old abandoned well in the mountains.``
``...It must have been a secret escape route, built by some past head of the Sonozaki family.``
``Most likely.@k@vS02/11/130700442. ...Probably constructed sometime after the Meiji era.@k@vS02/11/130700443. Still... it's impressive they managed to build something like that.``
``.........And everyone's remains... were at the bottom...?``
``Yes.@k@vS02/11/130700445. ...Below the escape tunnel, even deeper down, there was a pool of tar-like sludge.@k@vS02/11/130700446. They'd been tossed in without ceremony.``
``Given the height they fell from, the remains were completely submerged.@k@vS02/11/130700448. ...Recovering the bodies was an ordeal.``
Please... let them not find Rika-chan and Satoko, at least.
...That was the prayer behind my question, but that fragile hope was shattered in an instant.

``Yes.@k@vS02/11/130700450. Every last one.@k@vS02/11/130700451. ...In fact, we also found skeletal remains estimated to be over a decade old.@k@vS02/11/130700452. At least three individuals, we believe.@k@vS02/11/130700453. ...The dark underbelly of Hinamizawa's history, I suppose.``
``Once we've drained all the muck... who knows how many more we'll find.@k@vS02/11/130700455. ...A frightening thought.``
...............That burden had been weighing on Mion's shoulders all along.

``...We're currently cross-referencing disappearances from Shishibone City over the past several decades.@k@vS02/11/130700457. ...People are saying this could finally resolve all those cold missing-persons cases.``
In the deep silence... a heavy sigh escaped him...
Ooishi-san reached for his cigarettes to fill the pause, then realized this was a no-smoking area and reluctantly tucked them back into his breast pocket.
``Oh, right.@k@vS02/11/130700459. ...This is a bit of a tangent, but...@k@vS02/11/130700460. you knew Rika Furude-san, correct?``
``Of course.@k@vS02/01/130101214. ...She was one of us.``
changeReason: Old says 'She's my friend' but 仲間 means 'one of our group/companions.'

TextENGNew: ``Did she ever mention anything about, say, regularly taking insulin injections for diabetes, or anything along those lines?``
..........??@k Rika-chan had diabetes???@k I had no idea what he was talking about.
``Actually, we found something in her skirt pocket.``

``A syringe.@k@vS02/11/130700464. It was damaged, so we couldn't identify the substance inside.@k@vS02/11/130700465. ...It seemed rather out of place, which piqued my interest.@k@vS02/11/130700466. ...You truly don't know anything about it?``
...I knew nothing about Rika-chan carrying a syringe around.
All manner of dark rumors had been whispered about the Sonozaki family. ...Among them, baseless claims that they were involved in illegal trafficking.
...Was he implying that Rika-chan was also suspected of being mixed up in something like that...?!
``No, no... that's not what I'm suggesting.``
``...It just strikes me as odd, no matter how you look at it... that she'd be carrying a syringe on a trip to borrow soy sauce.``
.....................I couldn't make heads or tails of any of it.
My head was spinning...@k ...Nausea was rising...............
``Are you all right?@k@vS02/11/130700473. Shall I call a nurse?``
``Well, that's all there was about the syringe.@k@vS02/11/130700475. It was just something that personally caught my attention.``

``...Rika-san was something of a village mascot, after all.@k@vS02/11/130700477. ...The village is apparently in turmoil right now.@k@vS02/11/130700478. ...Between the elderly zealots and the Sonozaki family who killed her.``
``...The Three Families' effective grip on the village is all but gone.``
``.........If someone were to revive the dam proposal now, with this kind of timing, the village would be abandoned for certain.``
``...I'm... not connected to Hinamizawa anymore.``
That wasn't really true.
...I only said it because hearing about Hinamizawa's decline was painful... and I wanted the conversation to end.
There was a knock at the door.
It cracked open, and Mom asked whether we were still talking.
``Oh dear!@k@vS02/11/130700482. Got a bit carried away with the chatter.@k@vS02/11/130700483. ...What a fix!``
...............I glanced at the clock.@k ...Five minutes had long since passed.
There was still something I desperately wanted to ask, so I told Mom to give us just a little more time.
changeReason: Old incorrectly says 'He told my mother'; new correctly says 'I told Mom.' JPN 俺が (I) clearly indicates Keiichi is the speaker/actor, not Ooishi.

TextENGNew: ``...All right?@k@vS02/00/132700036. Make it quick.``
Mom clearly couldn't help wondering what on earth a police detective had to discuss with her son.
``.........Perhaps we should do this another day.``
``...Um... Ooishi-san.@k@vS02/01/130101220. .........What's happening with Mion... right now?``
Once I had that answer, I intended to send him on his way.
``...............Before I tell you that, there's just one thing, Maebara-san.@k@vS02/11/130700486. ...Something I need you to answer with complete honesty.``
``...What is it?``

``...............I need you to answer this with absolute seriousness.@k@vS02/11/130700488. ...If you say something careless, it could be treated as obstruction of the investigation, and you could face charges.``
...At the very end, Ooishi-san turned intimidating.
...What the hell was this about?
``...Maebara-san.@k@vS02/11/130700490. ...Who do you believe pushed Shion-san from the balcony?``
``...........................It couldn't have been anyone but Mion, could it?``
``Then who stabbed you?``
...Ooishi-san gave a strained smile, then dabbed at his forehead with a sweat-stained handkerchief.
It should have been the answer he expected, yet something about it clearly didn't sit right with him.
``She pushed Shion off, and she stabbed me.@k@vS02/01/130101227. ...You yourself just agreed it was Mion's doing, didn't you?``
``...The basis for saying Mion killed Shion rests on the testimony of a man living next door.``

``...This neighbor was actually a member of an organized crime syndicate—Shion-san's bodyguard, in effect.@k@vS02/11/130700494. The leader among them was a veteran who'd looked after the Sonozaki sisters since they were small.``
...This is what the man testified.
The commotion he heard from the adjacent room was identical to the sisterly brawls he used to hear when the twins still lived together.@k ...No—he was certain of it.
He heard Mion and Shion hurling insults at each other, heard the clamor of them grappling.
But at first, he dismissed it as a hallucination brought on by her instability.
changeReason: Old says 'dementia' but 錯乱 means 'derangement/agitation' -- different medical concept.

TextENGNew: ...After all, this sort of outburst... had been a nightly occurrence.
But that particular night... it went on longer than usual, and he said he went to her room intending to give her a sedative.
By the time the building manager brought the key, the noise had died down.
Still, he figured he should at least check on her. When she didn't respond to his calls, they went ahead and unlocked the door.
The room was in shambles.
...Shion had been pushed from the balcony, so it had to have been Mion's doing...@k or so the conclusion went.
``...In other words, he only heard it through the wall.@k@vS02/11/130700496. What's more, this apartment complex is connected to their organization, so it has hardly any other residents.``
``So they were the only ones who heard anything.@k@vS02/11/130700498. ...To put it plainly, not a single person actually saw Mion Sonozaki.``
``Even without seeing her... isn't it obvious it was Mion...?!``

``Now, hold on.@k@vS02/11/130700500. ...What I'm getting at is this.@k@vS02/11/130700501. ...You are the only person who definitely laid eyes on Mion Sonozaki that night.@k@vS02/11/130700502. The other witness, Shion-san, is no longer alive.``
``............I don't really follow... what you're trying to say...``
Ooishi-san paused.
Completely forgetting the no-smoking rule, he pulled out his pack of cigarettes again.
Click. He flicked his lighter and lit one.@k ...Then exhaled a long plume of purple-grey smoke...
``......Were you really stabbed by Mion Sonozaki?``
The smoke Ooishi-san exhaled drifted away... like a thunderhead carried off by the wind.
``.........Ooishi-san, there's no smoking in here.@k@vS02/01/130101233. ...The nurses will have your head.``
``Actually... Mion Sonozaki was found at the bottom of that well, too.``

``Death by falling.@k@vS02/11/130700507. It appears she was trying to escape through the well's hidden passage, lost her footing on the ladder,@k@vS02/11/130700508. fell all the way to the bottom,@k@vS02/11/130700509. and broke her neck.@k@vS02/11/130700510. ...Yes.@k@vS02/11/130700511. The same as Shion-san.@k@vS02/11/130700512. A broken neck.``
``After knocking you unconscious that day, Mion Sonozaki tried to escape through the hidden well...@k@vS02/11/130700514. ...and lost her footing and died.``
``...As though summoned by the corpses of the other victims she'd thrown down there.``
``............... But... that doesn't add up, does it...?``
``The autopsy confirmed without question that she died on that day.@k@vS02/11/130700517. ...She didn't return there after attacking you and Shion-san.``
``...She was already dead by then.``
My mouth hung open... unable to close.
...That was absolutely... impossible.
...The Mion who stabbed me... was unmistakably Mion.
...It couldn't have been... anyone else...........
``Th-Then... then... wh-who attacked me and Shion...?!@k@vS02/01/130101238. Who...?!``
``Whoever attacked you... was someone who was Mion Sonozaki, yet was not Mion Sonozaki.``
......My mind was in complete disarray... I couldn't form a coherent thought...
``...Do you remember what happened to Miyo Takano-san?``
``...I believe I heard that she... was burned to death somewhere in the mountains...``

``It's been established that she was strangled first, then burned.@k@vS02/11/130700522. ...The prefectural police handled the autopsy.@k@vS02/11/130700523. And something about it doesn't quite add up.``

``...The preliminary autopsy results indicated she'd been dead for at least twenty-four hours.@k@vS02/11/130700525. Since that was contradictory, they apparently scrambled to revise the record to say she died that same day.``
...My head was already reeling from everything about Mion...@k and now he was telling me... there was still more...?
.........I couldn't follow the tangled threads of what Ooishi-san was telling me...
``This twenty-four-hour result, according to our own veteran criminologist...@k@vS02/11/130700527. is quite reliable.@k@vS02/11/130700528. ...So what would that imply?``
``...I know I've asked this many times before, but...@k@vS02/11/130700530. on the night of Watanagashi...@k@vS02/11/130700531. you were with Miyo Takano-san, weren't you?``
``And you also saw her the day before, during the festival preparations, correct?@k@vS02/11/130700533. I ran into her then as well.``
``If she'd been dead for twenty-four hours... that would mean she was already dead the night before the festival.``
``...She was alive during the festival preparations.``
``But... by the time you all snuck into the ritual storehouse... Takano-san was already dead.``
The catalyst.@k The beginning of everything.
...The break-in at the ritual storehouse... which I had come to regret more times than I could count.
The one who had lured us there...............................Takano-san.
When she invited us... into the ritual storehouse... she was already... no longer among the living...
``There are far too many dead people walking around in this case.@k@vS02/11/130700538. ...Ha-ha-ha...``
...............An incident set in motion by the dead... and brought to a close by the dead.

``...Apparently, there's pressure to revise Mion Sonozaki's time of death—not to the day of the incident, but to right after she stabbed you.@k@vS02/11/130700540. ...Outside pressure, no doubt, to wrap this case up and be done with it.``
``...So I'd ask you to keep everything I've told you strictly to yourself.``
Ooishi-san grabbed the jacket he'd draped over his chair, stubbed out his cigarette on the sole of his shoe, and began waving his hand to disperse the lingering smoke.
``I've overstayed my welcome.``
``...I came here hoping to at least put this incident to rest for you, but...@k@vS02/11/130700544. ...it seems I've only muddied the waters.``
Still dazed... unable to muster a single word... I could only watch as he walked toward the door.
``......If something should come up... that you want to discuss, call me anytime.@k@vS02/11/130700546. My business card is tucked in with those souvenirs.``
``...Next time, I'll bring even better gifts when I visit.@k@vS02/11/130700548. Heh-heh-heh!``
Ooishi-san... seemed convinced I was still hiding something.
After everything that had happened... what could I possibly be hiding...?
I was the one things were being kept from.@k ...I was the one desperate for answers.
``Well then, until we meet again.@k@vS02/11/130700550. Take care of yourself.``
The door shut.
I listened... to the steady clip of Ooishi-san's footsteps... receding down the corridor.@k ...I still couldn't pull myself out of my stupor.
He'd come to put this incident to rest for me...?
What part of it was over?
Nothing was over.
...The incident... was still... going on...
......Then, slowly... an arm caked in fresh blood... emerged from beneath the bed.
...I wasn't all that surprised.
...This was... just a hallucination, born of my terror...
...Kei-chan... I told you, didn't I...
A rasping voice reached me from under the bed.@k ...Even so, I recognized it as Mion's.
``...What is it, Mion?@k@vS02/01/130101248. ...What did you tell me...?``
...I... warned you that day...
...From today onward... even if you see me...... it will be a demon... possessing my corpse......

``...Ahaha... yeah... you did say that, didn't you?@k@vS02/01/130101250. ...Sorry.@k@vS02/01/130101251. ...I wasn't careful enough.@k@vS02/01/130101252. ...That's why I got stabbed.@k@vS02/01/130101253. ...Ahahaha...``
Perhaps injured when she fell from the ladder...@k several of her fingernails... were cracked open and half torn off...
Her arm... crept slowly across the bed... searching for mine.
``Ah... hey... Mion.@k@vS02/01/130101255. ...I just thought of a joke.@k@vS02/01/130101256. ...Want to hear it?``
...........................The Mion beneath the bed gave no answer.
``S-Say that... I died here today.@k@vS02/01/130101258. ...But... starting tomorrow, I'd still be right here, going about my life as usual.``

``...And then... later, when Ooishi found out... once again, the time of death wouldn't match up.@k@vS02/01/130101260. He'd throw a fit, going on about how someone who should be dead was walking around...@k@vS02/01/130101261. Takano-san, then Mion, and me—number three.``
``...Right?!@k@vS02/01/130101263. Funny, isn't it?@k@vS02/01/130101264. Makes you laugh, doesn't it?@k@vS02/01/130101265. Hahahahahahahahahahaha...!``
``...Laugh.@k@vS02/01/130101267. ...Or was it... n-not that funny...?``
Mion's blood-soaked hand... groped blindly... and found my wrist.
Even with her nails torn away... she dug in as though clawing me... gripping with crushing force......
``Ow... ow ow... T-Take it easy...... Ow... that hurts...``
...Kei-chan.................. I've come to get you......
I tried to wrench my wrist free... but the strength pinning it was inhuman. I couldn't break loose.
She was only gripping one wrist... yet somehow my entire body was paralyzed.
...I couldn't move a muscle.
My fingers were splayed open... I couldn't even form a fist......
.........That... inability to even clench my fist... brought the memory of the restraining table flooding back............!
Blinding light. As if I were strapped to an operating table, searing light beating down on me!
I felt... the jagged edge of a nail pressing against the tip of my left pinky.
...Mion's forehead was split open... and with a face slathered in blood... she grinned...
...Nothing was over. ...Nothing was over.
This incident was nowhere near finished.
...It was still going on.
It was far from over.
Someone, please—put an end to this.
This cruel, wretched, pitiable, sorrowful incident...@rPlease, let it end.
That alone... is my only wish...
``I granted you one wish before, remember?@k@vS02/03/130300752. No more this time.@k@vS02/03/130300753. ...Ah-hahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahahaha!!!``
Mion pressed the nail firmly against the tip of my pinky.
...The hammer in her other hand rose slowly, arcing high into the air..................
